title: AWS Lambda to Amazon DynamoDB with Amazon SQS Partial Batch Error Handling
description: Process SQS messages with Lambda, write to DynamoDB, and handle partial batch failures with ReportBatchItemFailures and a Dead Letter Queue.

introBox.headline: How it works
text: This pattern demonstrates best-practice error handling when processing SQS messages with Lambda and writing to DynamoDB.
The Lambda function uses ReportBatchItemFailures to return only failed message IDs, so successfully processed messages are not retried.
Messages that fail after 3 retries are automatically routed to a Dead Letter Queue for investigation.
IAM permissions follow least-privilege: the Lambda function has only DynamoDBCrudPolicy scoped to the specific table.

bullets.text: Using Lambda with Amazon SQS — Reporting batch item failures
Error handling and automatic retries in AWS Lambda

text: See the GitHub repo for detailed testing instructions.

authors.name: Ajaya Shrestha
authors.bio: Cloud Support Engineer at AWS specializing in Lambda and serverless architectures.
